meta.notes: Stats shown where available from wiki. Many Transport/Heavy/Siege ships have incomplete stats on the wiki (marked with null). Ships from B18 update (Southampton, Iberia, Three Hierarchs, Montanes) are early access and may not yet be on the wiki.

shipClasses.name: Fast
shipClasses.description: Ships best suited for ranged and mobile combat. Require maximum focus and do not forgive mistakes. In skilled hands, they can win almost any battle.

shipClasses.name: Combat
shipClasses.description: The backbone of battle formations. Versatile and reliable in any situation. Have no strong advantages or drawbacks.

shipClasses.name: Transport
shipClasses.description: Best cargo capacity among all classes. Combat usage is secondary.

shipClasses.name: Heavy
shipClasses.description: A class of ships capable of withstanding heavy enemy fire. High firepower and durability come at the cost of tardiness.

shipClasses.name: Siege
shipClasses.description: Sail ships with powerful mortar weapons. Siege ships are nimble but fragile. A serious threat to forts, coastal defenses, and stationary ships.

shipClasses.name: Imperial
shipClasses.description: Various ships captured from the Inhagger Empire. More a classification by origin than by function.

shipbuildingSchools.name: Imperial
shipbuildingSchools.description: Inhagger Empire origin — no faction discount

ships.name: Axel Thorsen
ships.notes: Speed buffed from 9.5 to 9.8 in B18

ships.name: Iberia
ships.notes: Added in B18, one of the pioneers of the Espaniol shipbuilding school. Used for patrols and raiding.

ships.name: Firestorm
ships.notes: Premium PvP beast

ships.name: Red Arrow
ships.notes: Speed nerfed from 9.1 to 8.6 in B18

ships.name: Le Saint Louis
ships.notes: Was early access, becoming craftable in B18

ships.name: Montanes
ships.notes: Added in B18, 74-gun Spanish ship of the line. Battle class, rank II.

ships.name: Neptuno
ships.notes: Premium PvE farming ship, large crew ideal for boarding

ships.name: De Zeven Provincien
ships.notes: Also referred to as 'Zev' by community

ships.name: Sovereign
ships.notes: British ship of the line, most powerful 17th century warship inspiration

ships.name: Victory
ships.notes: HMS Victory, craftable Rate I legend

ships.name: Southampton
ships.notes: Added in B18, British 32-gun frigate. Transport, rate V.

ships.name: Friedrich Wilhelm
ships.notes: Was early access, becoming craftable in B18

ships.name: Constitution
ships.notes: USS Constitution, popular heavy line milestone

ships.name: Three Hierarchs
ships.notes: Added in B18, Russian fleet battleship, excellent maneuverability. Heavy, rate IV.

ships.name: Azov
ships.notes: Was early access, becoming craftable in B18

ships.name: Bellona
ships.notes: Monster in arenas per community

ships.name: Vasa
ships.notes: Maneuverability nerfed from 68 to 64 in B18

ships.name: 12 Apostolov
ships.notes: Considered the best first rate ship per community

ships.name: Santisima Trinidad
ships.notes: Largest sailing ship by gun count. Premium fleet dominator. Deletes PvE in 2-3 salvos.

ships.name: Deadfish
ships.notes: Speed nerfed 6.8→6.6, armor 8.2→8.0 in B18. Special ability 'Rage': gains defense/damage/accuracy after taking damage.

types.description: Standard short- to mid-range artillery with balanced performance. Reliable in most situations.
High-range cannons with improved precision. Ideal for long-range engagements.
Short-range, high-damage weapons that excel in close combat. Higher max damage but slower reload and shorter range.
Specialized weapons for bow and stern positions.
Devastating arc-fire damage against ships, perfect against forts and coastal defenses. Has minimum range.
Exotic or unique weapons with special firing behavior or effects.

tiers.description: Entry-level weapons purchased for Gold. No crafting required. Ideal for early-game.
Upgraded and specialized equipment. Must be crafted using materials. Better stats than Basic.
Most powerful weapons. Generally outperform Basic and Advanced tiers.
tiers.acquisition: Purchase with Pirate Tokens, weapon convoys, game modes

ammunition.name: Round Shots
ammunition.description: Default cannonball. Higher max damage and longer range. Best all-around ammo.

ammunition.name: Heated Shots
ammunition.description: Burning cannonball that applies damage over time (fire). Good against unarmored targets and port structures.
ammunition.notes: Ideal for sustained DPS with regular cannons

ammunition.name: Grapeshot
ammunition.description: Anti-crew shot for close range. Reduces enemy crew numbers before boarding. Faster reload but lower range.
ammunition.notes: Sweet spot aiming required — hit crew on deck without hitting hull

ammunition.name: Bar Shots
ammunition.description: Chain & balls that slow ships by hitting sails. Useful for chasing or preventing escape.
ammunition.notes: Less effective if opponents pull sails

ammunition.name: Snowballs
ammunition.description: Christmas event ammunition. Only damages Sea Elves NPS. Covers opponent's screen with snow when used on other targets. Melts in southern regions.

tacticalPayloads.name: Small Gunpowder Barrels
tacticalPayloads.description: Explosive barrels deployed from stern. Area denial against pursuers.

tacticalPayloads.name: Large Phosphorous Mine
tacticalPayloads.description: Large phosphorous mine for area denial.

tacticalPayloads.name: Large Shrapnel Mines
tacticalPayloads.description: Large shrapnel mine for area damage.

tacticalPayloads.name: Fire Ship
tacticalPayloads.description: An entire ship set ablaze and sent toward enemies.

slots.description: Required to be a type of sail. Affects ship speed.
Typically used for Combat Crow's Nest or similar.
Typically used for Cellars or similar utility upgrade.
Unlocked by accumulating experience while commanding the ship.
Experience starts counting only after slot 4 is unlocked.

slots.unlockMethod: All five ship class branches (Fast, Combat, Heavy, Transport, Siege) must be fully researched
slots.description: Extremely grindy to unlock. Vision/detection upgrades recommended.

items.name: Tarpulin Sails
items.cost: Gold + Voodoo Skulls (high-rate ships exempt per B18)
items.notes: Best sails. Low-rate ships no longer require Voodoo Skulls per B18

items.name: Combat Crow's Nest
items.effect: Increased visibility range, aim speed increase
items.notes: Excellent for spotting aggressive spyglass ships

items.name: Cellars
items.effect: Additional cargo hold space, prevents goods from perishing
items.notes: Great for early game sailing in southern waters where perishing is an issue

items.name: Small Hooks
items.effect: Increased boarding range, item collection speed, fishing speed

items.name: Advanced Gun Carriages
items.effect: Increased weapon angle and aiming speed

items.name: Extra Sails
items.notes: Also referred to as Small/Large Additional Sails

notes.durability: Upgrades have effective durability that must be periodically repaired (small gold cost). If it breaks, repair cost is significantly higher.
notes.costScaling: Upgrade costs drastically increase with ship rate. Rate VII: 300-600 gold. Rate IV: same gold + Voodoo Skulls (3) + Battle Marks (20-30) + Rum (150-250).
notes.gradeSystem: Upgrades are most valuable at grade 2 or higher. Grade 1 may not justify cost.
notes.b18Changes: B18 removed Battle Mark requirements from most upgrades. Best sails on low-ranking ships no longer require Voodoo Skulls.

consumables.name: Field Kit
consumables.description: Field repair kit for at-sea repairs.

consumables.name: Phosphorous
consumables.description: Phosphorous material used for special ammunition and consumables.

consumables.name: Smoke Screen
consumables.description: Larger smoke screen for area concealment.

rawMaterials.name: Wood
rawMaterials.description: Primary construction material. Gathered from Wood Mines on personal island.

rawMaterials.name: Iron Ore
rawMaterials.description: Raw iron ore. Must be refined into Iron.

rawMaterials.name: Copper Ore
rawMaterials.description: Raw copper ore. Refined into Copper/Bronze.

rawMaterials.name: Coal
rawMaterials.description: Fuel for smelting. Essential for endgame.

rawMaterials.name: Fish
rawMaterials.description: Gathered from fishing nodes at sea. Can be traded to Ferryman merchants for resources. Spoils quickly.

rawMaterials.name: Resin
rawMaterials.description: Raw material gathered from gathering points.

rawMaterials.name: Wreckage
rawMaterials.description: Salvaged from shipwrecks and destroyed ships.

rawMaterials.name: Provision
rawMaterials.notes: Running out in northern waters kills crew; in southern waters just reduces effectiveness.

processedMaterials.name: Iron
processedMaterials.description: Refined iron. Core material for hulls, guns, upgrades, and builds.

processedMaterials.name: Bronze
processedMaterials.description: Alloy of copper and tin. Used for advanced repairs and equipment.

processedMaterials.name: Canvas
processedMaterials.notes: Can be crafted at any port workshop

processedMaterials.name: Bulkhead
processedMaterials.description: Ship structural component. Best crafted on personal island for 20% discount.

processedMaterials.name: Rum
processedMaterials.description: Processed consumable and upgrade material. Required for high-rate ship upgrades.

craftingNotes.personalIslandDiscount: 20% discount on crafting at personal island (bulkheads, planks, plates, heavy items)
craftingNotes.factionPortDiscount: 25% discount on ship crafting materials at matching faction ports
craftingNotes.productionLimit: Port crafting has production/manpower limits (300 → 600 → 1000 with skill upgrades)

currencies.name: Gold
currencies.description: Primary currency. Used for purchases, upgrades, repairs.

currencies.name: Battle Marks
currencies.description: Combat currency. Required for some upgrades and unlock slots. DO NOT SELL — irreplaceable.

currencies.name: Voodoo Skulls
currencies.description: Rare currency for top-tier upgrades and Imperial ships. DO NOT SELL — irreplaceable.

currencies.name: Escudos
currencies.description: Special currency for Imperial content. DO NOT SELL — irreplaceable.

currencies.name: Seahorses
currencies.description: Christmas event currency. Exchangeable at overseas traders. Removed after event ends.

currencies.name: Chest
currencies.description: Loot container. Imperial Chest has 3.6% chance for premium ships.

tradeGoods.name: Sugar
tradeGoods.description: Trade commodity. Buy cheap in the north, sell at premium in the south.

tradeGoods.name: Salt
tradeGoods.description: Trade commodity. Buy cheap in the north, sell at premium in the south.

crew.overview: Crew participate in boarding battles, load guns, and help perform various actions. Ships with large crews are harder to board or disable.

types.name: Sailors
types.role: Ship operations — reload speed, maneuverability, work speed
types.notes: Reducing sailor count penalizes work speed but frees slots for combat crew

types.name: Musketeers
types.role: Boarding combat — offensive power in boarding actions
types.notes: More musketeers = more deadly boarding offense

types.name: Special Crew
types.role: Unique crew members with special bonuses
types.notes: Can be obtained randomly when acquiring rumors at taverns

mechanics.satiety: Crew must be fed. Running out in northern waters kills crew; southern waters only reduces effectiveness.
mechanics.overloading: Can overload boarding party with extra crew from rescues or port. Limited by ship capacity.
mechanics.boarding: Ship with more crew usually wins boarding. Grapeshot reduces enemy crew before boarding.
mechanics.crewUpgrades: Crew size can be increased with upgrades and special crew members.

factions.name: Imperial (Inhagger Empire)
factions.description: Enemy NPC faction. Imperial ships can be captured at Corsa-Nois Bay.

skills.name: Fast Travel
skills.description: Port-to-port teleportation. Free once every 2 hours with skill upgrade.

skills.name: Escort
skills.description: Manage captured ship swarms without headaches.

skills.name: Critical
skills.description: Damage spike when cannons are maxed.

skills.name: Captured Ship Limit
skills.description: Up to 3 captured ships with you.

skills.name: Ship Dismantling Bonus
skills.description: Improves wood and fabric gains from dismantling.

skills.name: Post-Boarding Repair
skills.description: Repairs your ship after successful boarding.

skills.name: Captured Ship Repair
skills.description: Repairs captured ship by 50% of max HP.

captains.name: Bartholomew
captains.notes: Match captain bonuses to ship playstyle

islandBuildings.name: Plate Workshop
islandBuildings.description: Produces plates. First building priority — everything else needs plates.

islandBuildings.name: Pier
islandBuildings.description: Dock fleets at island. Reduces port runs.

islandBuildings.name: Mission Hall (Outpost)
islandBuildings.description: Cash engine. Auto-pulls from mines within 7000m. 50 manpower capacity.

islandBuildings.name: Lighthouse
islandBuildings.description: Adds island to Fast Travel network.
